hook: Values only help when they are specific enough to guide behavior under pressure.
body: Saying you value self-respect means little until it shapes what you do with mixed signals, flakiness, and temptation.

hook: Some men claim values in the abstract and abandon them in the first emotionally charged moment.
body: A value becomes real when it costs you something and you keep it anyway.

body: If respect matters, you do not keep begging clarity from someone who keeps treating you carelessly.
